rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JAMES B. MCCLATCHY TRUST DATED NOVEMBER 15, 1974
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Adjudicattion On Count One For Declaratory Relief Re The Meaning Of The 1987 Stockholders' Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Carlos McClatchy's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. In his prayer for relief, Mr. McClatchy requests a declaration that the Stockholders' Agreement permits the Co-Trustees to "liquidate all the COMPANY's shares of Class B common stock held in the TRUST, diversify the TRUST's assets, and make income-producing investments[.]" The Stockholders' Agreement, however, does not govern diversification of Trust assets or the Co-Trustees' duties as to investing Trust assets. Interpreting the Stockholders' Agreement on its own, the Court cannot determine the ways in which the Co-Trustees can properly exercise their discretion. The declaration Mr. McClatchy seeks intertwines the Stockholders' Agreement and the Trust in such a way that summary adjudication is inappropriate. Unlike Hood and Southern California Edison, this case does not concern the effect of declaratory relief on other causes of action. Instead, the issue is that the relief sought within the declaratory relief cause of action itself goes beyond and would not be fully resolved by the narrower contract interpretation question presented in this motion. Moreover, as an independent basis for denial, the Co-Trustees' statement in the October 14, 2011 letter that the terms of the Stockholders' Agreement "prevent" them from diversifying the Trust assets presents a disputed issue of material fact with respect to the meaning and intent behind the word "prevent." Given the evidence presented on this motion, Mr. McClatchy's material fact number 5 in his Separate Statement is not undisputed. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so advise all other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Wednesday, January 21, 2015. Co-Trustees' counsel is directed to submit a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling and compliant with CCP section 437c(g).
